title: macrococculus pomifer
intro: Macrococculus pomifer, also known as the Prickly Ash tree, is a species of tree in the family Rutaceae, native to the southeastern United States and Cuba. The bark and roots of the Macrococculus pomifer tree have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to relieve pain, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Macrococculus pomifer include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve headaches and arthritis, and even help to lower blood pressure. Macrococculus pomifer can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
